additional_rules.name: mass-assignment-without-strong-params
additional_rules.message: Raw params hash passed to create/update/new/build — mass-assignment vulnerability. Use strong parameters.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Define a private method `def post_params; params.require(:post).permit(:title, :body); end` and call `Post.create(post_params)`. Never pass `params[:post]` directly.

additional_rules.name: params-permit-bang
additional_rules.message: params.permit!/to_unsafe_h disables strong-parameters whitelist — mass-assignment vulnerability.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Replace with `params.require(:resource).permit(:attr1, :attr2)`. If you genuinely need every key (rare), document the security review and pin the controller in CODEOWNERS.

additional_rules.name: raw-sql-string-interpolation
additional_rules.message: String interpolation inside where/find_by_sql/exec_query — SQL injection risk.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Use hash conditions `where(name: name)` or parameterized strings `where('name = ?', name)`. For find_by_sql use `sanitize_sql_array(['... ?', value])`.

additional_rules.name: skip-csrf-without-token-auth
additional_rules.message: Unscoped skip_before_action :verify_authenticity_token — CSRF disabled for the entire controller.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Scope with `only: %i[create update destroy]` and confirm those actions authenticate via Authorization header (token), not session cookies. API-only controllers should inherit from ActionController::API instead.

additional_rules.name: html-safe-on-user-input
additional_rules.message: html_safe on view-layer string — XSS risk when the source is user-controlled.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Use `sanitize(string, tags: %w[a strong em], attributes: %w[href])` with an explicit allowlist. html_safe is appropriate only for trusted constants.

additional_rules.name: update-all-without-scope-confirmation
additional_rules.message: update_all/delete_all skips callbacks AND validations — confirm scope and absence of required side effects before use.
additional_rules.fix_hint: If callbacks are required: iterate with `find_each(&:save)` or extract to a job. If skipping is intentional, add a comment explaining why and confirm the relation is scoped (`Post.where(active: false).update_all(...)`).

additional_rules.name: deliver-now-in-controller
additional_rules.message: Mailer.deliver_now blocks the request thread on SMTP — use deliver_later (ActiveJob).
additional_rules.fix_hint: Replace `WelcomeMailer.with(user: u).welcome_email.deliver_now` with `.deliver_later`. ActiveJob (solid_queue on Rails 8) handles retries and async delivery.

additional_rules.name: loop-over-relation-without-includes
additional_rules.message: Iterating over a Relation without explicit eager-loading — verify includes()/preload() to prevent N+1.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Add `.includes(:author, :tags)` to the query before iterating. Consider `find_each` for batched iteration over large collections.

additional_rules.name: before-filter-removed
additional_rules.message: before_filter/after_filter/around_filter were removed in Rails 5.1 — runtime error on Rails 5.1+.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Replace with before_action/after_action/around_action/skip_before_action. Same arguments and semantics.

additional_rules.name: update-attributes-removed
additional_rules.message: update_attributes/update_attributes! were deprecated in Rails 6.1 and removed in Rails 7.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Replace with .update / .update! (identical signature). The bang variant raises on validation failure.

additional_rules.name: fat-controller-action
additional_rules.message: Controller action exceeds ~40 lines — extract business logic to a service object or form object.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Move workflow into `app/services/{resource}/{action}.rb`. Controllers should: parse strong params, call the service, render or redirect.

additional_rules.name: default-scope-discouraged
additional_rules.message: default_scope hides filtering at every call site and breaks unscoped/count semantics.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Replace with explicit scopes (`scope :published, -> { where.not(published_at: nil) }`) and call them at use sites. Use `Post.unscoped` only as a last resort.

additional_rules.name: missing-foreign-key-index
additional_rules.message: t.references without explicit index — Rails 5+ adds an index by default but legacy migrations may miss it.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Use `t.references :user, index: true, foreign_key: true` (or `add_reference :posts, :user, index: true, foreign_key: true` for stand-alone migrations). foreign_key enforces referential integrity at the DB level.

additional_rules.name: puts-or-print-in-app-code
additional_rules.message: puts/print/pp/p in application code — use Rails.logger.{debug,info,warn,error} instead.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Rails.logger.info("event", user_id: user.id). Configure structured logging (lograge or rails-semantic_logger) for JSON output in production.

additional_boundaries.name: Controllers must not query models with ad-hoc where/joins (push to query objects)
Models must not reference controllers, request, params, or session
